companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: NVIDIA's Annual Meeting of Stockholders was held June 24; proxy voting results (Form 8-K) are due June 30 — 2 days away. Vera Rubin is in full production and powering 35 AI HPC systems across European institutions. NVIDIA runs 81% of the TOP500 supercomputers and 90% of new entries. The BioNeMo Agent Toolkit launched at ISC High Performance 2026 in Hamburg for life sciences AI pipelines.

items.headline: NVIDIA Annual Meeting held June 24; proxy results (Form 8-K) due June 30
items.detail: NVIDIA held its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders online on June 24 at 9 a.m. PT. Stockholders voted on 10 director elections, executive compensation, PwC ratification for fiscal 2027, and four shareholder proposals. Final voting results will be filed in a current report on Form 8-K by June 30 — 2 days away. FY2026 highlights from the proxy: revenue $215.9 billion (+65% YoY), gross margin 71.1%, operating income $130.4 billion, diluted EPS $4.90 (+67%).

items.headline: NVIDIA runs 81% of TOP500 supercomputers; Vera Rubin powers 35 European HPC systems at ISC Hamburg
items.detail: At ISC High Performance 2026 in Hamburg, NVIDIA announced a record 35 AI HPC systems using the Vera Rubin platform are in development across European research institutions. NVIDIA technology runs 81% of the TOP500 supercomputers, with 90% of systems new to the list powered by NVIDIA, and 26 TOP500 systems now using NVIDIA Grace CPUs — up eight from the prior list. The Vera Rubin platform is purpose-built for global-scale AI infrastructure from cloud to manufacturing.

items.headline: BioNeMo Agent Toolkit launched for life sciences AI pipelines; warm-water cooling eliminates data center cooling water use
items.detail: NVIDIA announced the BioNeMo Agent Toolkit at ISC High Performance 2026, enabling AI agents, scientists, and labs to gather evidence, reason across findings, run computational experiments, and recommend next best steps to accelerate scientific discovery. NVIDIA also unveiled a warm-water cooling system that eliminates cooling-loop water use entirely — a potential 100% reduction in cooling water consumption for AI data centers.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Google DeepMind suffered its worst talent week of 2026: five senior researchers departed in one week — Nobel laureate John Jumper (to Anthropic), Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer (reportedly to OpenAI), and Jonas Adler, Alexander Pritzel, and Arthur Conmy (all to Anthropic). Alphabet lost an estimated $270 billion in market cap across two trading sessions. Gemini 3.5 Pro's self-imposed June 30 deadline is 2 days away at roughly 50% prediction market odds. Gemini 3.5 Flash launched for agents and coding; Gemini Omni was announced.

items.headline: Five senior DeepMind researchers depart in one week — Alphabet loses ~$270B in market cap
items.detail: Google DeepMind's worst talent week of 2026: Nobel Prize winner John Jumper (AlphaFold2 co-creator, to Anthropic), Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer (reportedly to OpenAI), and researchers Jonas Adler, Alexander Pritzel, and Arthur Conmy (all to Anthropic) all announced departures within a single week. Alphabet's stock fell roughly 7% across two trading sessions, erasing approximately $270 billion in market capitalization. The losses coincide with the pending Gemini 3.5 Pro launch delay.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro June 30 deadline 2 days away at ~50% prediction market odds; may slip to July
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro — Google's next frontier model with Sundar Pichai's self-imposed June 30 window — remains in limited Vertex AI enterprise preview as of June 28. Prediction markets sit at approximately 50% odds for a June 30 launch. CryptoBriefing reported insider indications of a potential July slip, which would be Google DeepMind's first publicly missed model window this year. Expected specs: 2M token context, Deep Think reasoning, pricing approximately $15 per million input tokens.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Flash launched for agents and coding; Gemini Omni announced for multimodal generation
items.detail: Google DeepMind launched Gemini 3.5 Flash for agents, coding, and complex long-horizon tasks, delivering frontier performance at lower cost ahead of the Gemini 3.5 Pro full launch. Gemini Omni was announced as a new model for world understanding, multimodality, and editing — designed to generate any output from any input, beginning with video. These two models are part of Google's June 2026 rollout cadence ahead of the flagship Gemini 3.5 Pro.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Copilot Cowork Frontier grace period ends July 1 — 3 days away. Microsoft unveiled MAI-Code-1 and MAI-Code-1-Flash at Build 2026 for code generation alongside the 35B-parameter MAI-Thinking-1 reasoning model. Majorana 2 quantum chip achieves 20-second average qubit lifetime and 1,000x better reliability than the previous generation. Work IQ APIs and Web IQ are GA. Microsoft and Anthropic remain in active Maia 200 chip negotiations.

items.headline: Copilot Cowork Frontier grace period ends July 1 — 3 days away; configure usage-based billing or lose access
items.detail: Microsoft Copilot Cowork reached general availability June 16 under Copilot Credits usage-based billing. Frontier preview tenants have a grace period until July 1 — now 3 days away — before billing activates and access is cut off for unconfigured organizations. Microsoft IQ (Work IQ APIs + Web IQ) is GA across GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Foundry, and Copilot Studio. Microsoft 365 Business Standard and Business Premium with Copilot formalize as permanent commercial SKUs on July 1.

items.headline: MAI-Code-1 and MAI-Code-1-Flash launched at Build 2026; MAI-Thinking-1 (35B) for enterprise agents
items.detail: Microsoft unveiled MAI-Code-1 — its inaugural model generating source code from written descriptions, tuned for GitHub and VS Code — alongside MAI-Code-1-Flash (faster, lower cost). MAI-Thinking-1, a 35B-parameter reasoning model, targets enterprise agentic workflows. Seven new MAI models in total were announced at Build 2026. Azure Cobalt 200 VMs deliver 50% performance improvement for modern agentic AI workloads.

items.headline: Majorana 2 quantum chip achieves 20-second qubit lifetime and 1,000x reliability improvement
items.detail: Microsoft's Majorana 2 quantum chip — announced at Build 2026 — achieves an average qubit lifetime of 20 seconds with instances up to a minute, representing a 1,000x reliability improvement over the previous generation. This is described as a giant step toward scale for fault-tolerant quantum computing. Separately, Microsoft is exploring fine-tuned open-source model alternatives (such as DeepSeek) for some Copilot Cowork workloads to reduce API costs at agentic token scale — this does not affect Claude's position in flagship Foundry or enterprise Copilot products.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: OpenAI launched GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna in limited preview on June 26 under U.S. government-imposed access restrictions. GPT-4.5 was retired the same day. Codex Remote reached GA on all ChatGPT plans. The confidential S-1 was filed June 8 with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan leading; the company is now leaning toward a 2027 IPO. A 42-state AG probe is ongoing. ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share fell below 50% for the first time.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna launched June 26 in limited preview under government restrictions; GPT-4.5 retired
items.detail: OpenAI launched three GPT-5.6 models on June 26 in limited preview under U.S. government-imposed access restrictions: Sol (flagship; $5/$30 per million input/output tokens), Terra (balanced; $2.50/$15), and Luna (fast and affordable; $1/$6). Sol is built for frontier reasoning and long-horizon agentic work; Terra matches GPT-5.5 performance at 2x lower cost; Luna is the fastest and most affordable. GPT-4.5 was retired June 26, replaced by GPT-5.5 in existing conversations.

items.headline: Codex Remote GA on all ChatGPT plans; memory controls updated with per-item deletion
items.detail: Codex Remote is now generally available on all ChatGPT plans, allowing users to start or continue work on a connected Mac or Windows host from the mobile app, review progress, and approve actions remotely. Memory controls were updated: users can now delete specific individual memories from the summary page and turn memory off entirely via a single action. These expand Codex and memory capabilities from enterprise to all subscribers.

items.headline: IPO leaning 2027; 42-state AG probe ongoing; ChatGPT global market share below 50% for first time
items.detail: OpenAI filed its confidential S-1 with the SEC on June 8 with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan leading. The company is now reportedly leaning toward a 2027 listing — CFO Sarah Friar cited operational readiness concerns, Stargate cash burn, and chilling effects from SpaceX IPO volatility. A coalition of 42 state AGs (led by NY AG Letitia James) opened a formal probe June 12 covering user harm, advertising practices, and model sycophancy. Sensor Tower found ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share at 46.4% — first time below 50% — with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: Mythos 5 was partially restored June 26 to approximately 100 companies and federal agencies under Commerce Secretary Lutnick's approval; Fable 5 remains suspended for general use (Day 16 as of June 28), with Axios reporting the Trump administration is close to allowing Fable access 'as soon as this coming week.' Anthropic identified its largest-ever distillation attack: Alibaba-linked Qwen operators used 25,000 fraudulent accounts for 28.8 million Claude exchanges. John Jumper (Nobel laureate) joins; AI for Science event June 30 — 2 days away. Series H ($30B+) closing; October 2026 Nasdaq IPO remains the target.

items.headline: Mythos 5 partially restored June 26 to ~100 companies and agencies; Fable 5 still suspended Day 16; broad access 'as soon as this coming week'
items.detail: Commerce Secretary Lutnick signed a letter June 26 permitting approximately 100 trusted companies and federal agencies to access Claude Mythos 5, including critical infrastructure operators and government users. Fable 5 remains suspended for general consumer and developer access on Day 16. Anthropic posted June 27 that it is restoring Mythos 5 access quickly and continuing talks to expand access and restore Fable 5. Axios reported June 27 that the Trump administration is close to allowing Fable 5 access 'as soon as this coming week,' pending Pentagon and NSA sign-off.

items.headline: Alibaba/Qwen operators ran 28.8 million fraudulent Claude exchanges via 25,000 accounts — largest distillation attack identified
items.detail: Anthropic accused Alibaba-linked operators of its Qwen AI lab of executing the largest distillation attack Anthropic has identified: approximately 25,000 fraudulent accounts conducted 28.8 million exchanges with Claude between April and June 2026, the apparent intent being to train competing models on Claude's outputs. The accusation was filed via Bloomberg. The attack highlights the scale of unauthorized API use for model distillation and Anthropic's investment in fraud detection.

items.headline: Nobel laureate John Jumper joins from DeepMind; AI for Science event June 30 (2 days); Series H $30B+ closing
items.detail: John Jumper — 2024 Nobel Prize winner in Chemistry and AlphaFold2 co-creator — joins Anthropic after departing Google DeepMind, appearing first at 'The Briefing: AI for Science' virtual event June 30 at 10 AM PST — 2 days away. Claude Code added /rewind (resume conversations before /clear), cut CPU use during streaming ~37%, and improved MCP reliability and OAuth retries. Anthropic is closing a Series H round exceeding $30 billion at a valuation above $900 billion, targeting an October 2026 Nasdaq IPO at a projected ~$1.1 trillion first-day market cap.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta is building an AI-powered prediction market standalone app called Arena (codenamed 'Antwerp'/'FBForecast') using Llama to auto-generate questions and resolve markets. More than 1,600 Meta employees signed a petition against a program that monitors their clicks and keystrokes for AI training data. Meta Glasses (launched June 23) continue to show strong early demand; AI Mode is live on Facebook. AMD MI450 samples shipping; CoreWeave $21B agreement through December 2032 on track.

items.headline: Meta building 'Arena' AI prediction market app powered by Llama — codenamed 'Antwerp'/'FBForecast'
items.detail: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg directed a team to build a standalone prediction market app called Arena (internal codenames: 'Antwerp' and 'FBForecast') that uses Llama to automatically generate questions from trending topics, make personalized market recommendations to users, and resolve markets via AI. The app competes with Kalshi, Polymarket, and Manifold. No public launch date has been announced.

items.headline: 1,600+ Meta employees petition against AI monitoring of clicks and keystrokes for training data
items.detail: More than 1,600 Meta employees company-wide signed a petition protesting a program that monitors their clicks and keystrokes to generate training data for AI models, raising privacy and autonomy concerns. The move comes as Meta continues to expand the Llama open-weights model family and internal AI tooling while facing internal pushback on AI-first workplace changes.

items.headline: Meta Glasses strong post-launch demand; AI Mode live on Facebook; 2026 capex $115–135B unchanged
items.detail: Meta's in-house Meta Glasses (launched June 23 with EssilorLuxottica at $299–$399) show strong early sales momentum with smart glasses shipments up 167% YoY in Q1 2026 and Meta at 69.2% market share. AI Mode launched on Facebook, pulling from public posts across Groups and Reels via a three-tier model routing system. Meta's 2026 capex guidance of $115–135 billion remains unchanged, supporting the AMD 6GW partnership, CoreWeave $21B agreement, and AI infrastructure buildout.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: Amazon is eyeing a $3 trillion valuation as AWS AI revenue accelerates. Bedrock AgentCore's optimization update added failure, intent, and trajectory insights and is now SOC 1/2/3 compliant. G7 instances with NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell deliver 4.6x AI inference performance over G6. AWS Continuum for code vulnerabilities is in gated preview; AWS Context (knowledge graph for agents) is coming soon. Project Rainier runs nearly 500K Trainium2 chips for Anthropic workloads.

items.headline: Bedrock AgentCore adds failure/intent/trajectory insights; SOC 1/2/3 compliant; Web Search GA
items.detail: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore received an optimization update adding failure insights, intent insights, and trajectory insights that turn production agent traces into continuous improvement feedback loops. Web Search on AgentCore reached general availability. The service is now SOC 1, 2, and 3 compliant. AWS Summit New York launches also included AWS Continuum (code vulnerability prioritization and auto-remediation in gated preview) and AWS Context (relationship knowledge graph enabling agentic search, coming soon).

items.headline: G7 instances with NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell deliver 4.6x AI inference performance; Grok 4.3 on Bedrock
items.detail: AWS is the first major cloud provider to support NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs via new G7 instances, delivering up to 4.6x AI inference performance compared to G6 instances. Grok 4.3 is available on Amazon Bedrock with tool calling, structured output, and response streaming, adding xAI to Bedrock's multi-model roster alongside Claude, GPT-5.5, and others.

items.headline: Amazon eyeing $3 trillion valuation; Project Rainier at ~500K Trainium2 chips running Anthropic workloads
items.detail: Amazon is targeting a $3 trillion market capitalization as AWS AI revenue growth accelerates through 2026. Project Rainier runs nearly half a million Trainium2 chips for Anthropic Claude workloads at scale, with Anthropic expecting 1M+ Trainium2 chips by year end under the $100B+/10-year, 5GW agreement. Amazon's $50 billion Stargate investment positions AWS as a core compute partner across the OpenAI infrastructure network alongside Oracle and CoreWeave.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: Grok 4.5 entered private beta at Tesla and SpaceX this week, built on the 1.5-trillion-parameter V9 model trained with Cursor data and posting performance reportedly close to Opus. SPCX at approximately $153 — still 32% below the $225.64 ATH from June 16. Grok adult content is generating more than 10 billion images monthly; xAI engineers publicly acknowledged CSAM is an unsolved technical problem. Reflection AI's $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts July 1 — 3 days away.

items.headline: Grok 4.5 enters private beta at Tesla and SpaceX — 1.5T parameters, V9 model, trained on Cursor data
items.detail: xAI's next-generation Grok 4.5 is running in private beta inside Tesla and SpaceX as of late June 2026. Elon Musk confirmed the deployment on X, noting early evaluations place performance close to, and potentially above, Opus. Grok 4.5 is built on xAI's V9 foundation model (1.5 trillion parameters, training completed May 26) and incorporates supplemental training on Cursor AI coding data — integrating assets from the $60B pending acquisition ahead of its formal Q3 2026 close.

items.headline: Grok adult content tops 10B images/month; xAI engineers admit CSAM has no current technical fix
items.detail: Grok's adult content generation capability is producing more than 10 billion images per month. xAI engineers publicly acknowledged that child sexual abuse material (CSAM) generation is an unsolved technical problem with no current fix — drawing criticism from child safety advocates and lawmakers. xAI has positioned explicit content as a deliberate product differentiator. This represents a regulatory and reputational risk for both xAI and its parent SpaceX ahead of the Cursor acquisition close.

items.headline: Reflection AI $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts July 1 (3 days); $60B Cursor acquisition toward Q3 close
items.detail: SpaceX's $6.3B compute agreement with Reflection AI starts July 1 — 3 days away — at $150M/month for NVIDIA GB300 GPU access via Colossus 2 in Memphis through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after the first 3 months. Both Colossus 1 (rented to Anthropic at ~$1.25B/month) and Colossus 2 (Reflection AI) are fully committed to third parties. SpaceX's $60B all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI) remains on track for Q3 2026 regulatory close; SpaceX and Cursor are jointly training a new model for Grok Build.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: OpenAI and Broadcom unveiled 'Jalapeño' on June 24 — OpenAI's first custom AI accelerator, designed in nine months for LLM inference, targeting H2 2026 Stargate deployment. Q2 AI semiconductor revenue was $10.8B (+143% YoY); Q3 guidance is approximately $16B. The $0.65/share quarterly dividend is payable June 30 — 2 days away. The $35B Apollo/Blackstone AI XPV Platform is operational, enabling 20GW+ of Broadcom XPU compute through 2028.

items.headline: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil Jalapeño — first custom OpenAI ASIC built in 9 months for LLM inference; H2 2026 Stargate target
items.detail: OpenAI and Broadcom unveiled Jalapeño on June 24 — OpenAI's first custom AI accelerator chip — co-developed from initial design to manufacturing tape-out in nine months, what the companies believe is the fastest ASIC development cycle ever achieved in high-performance advanced semiconductors. Jalapeño is a reticle-sized ASIC designed from the ground up for LLM inference, offering substantially better performance per watt than current state-of-the-art. OpenAI used its own AI models to accelerate chip design. Initial deployment targets H2 2026 in Stargate data centers.

items.headline: Q2 AI semiconductor revenue $10.8B (+143% YoY); $0.65 dividend payable June 30; Q3 guidance ~$16B
items.detail: Broadcom reported Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue of $10.8 billion (+143% YoY) on record quarterly total revenue of $22.2 billion, supported by multi-year supply agreements with Google, Meta, Microsoft, OpenAI, and Anthropic. Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance is approximately $16 billion. CEO Hock Tan described demand from Broadcom's six custom-silicon customers as 'simply insatiable.' The $0.65 per share quarterly cash dividend — marking 14 consecutive years of increases — is payable June 30, 2 days away.

items.headline: Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform operational — enables 20GW+ Broadcom XPU compute through 2028
items.detail: The Apollo and Blackstone-led $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform is operational, enabling more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. The platform facilitates Anthropic's 1+ gigawatt compute expansion at Fluidstack-based sites beginning mid-2026. Broadcom's Tomahawk 6 (Davisson) — the industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch with co-packaged optics — was unveiled mid-June as the networking layer for the next AI compute generation.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: PLTR recovered to approximately $112.15 on June 28 (range $107.88–$114.08) from the 52-week low of $107.27 on June 25, though still down roughly 35% year-to-date. AIPCon 10 showcased new enterprise partnerships with Kirkland & Ellis, McCarthy Building Companies, and GNP Seguros (Mexico). The Treasury Department contract (June 23), Army $10B/10-year deal, and DHS $1B/5-year deal anchor federal expansion. Mythos 5 partial restoration June 26 improves the Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline outlook.

items.headline: PLTR recovers to ~$112 from 52-week low; AIPCon 10 announces Kirkland & Ellis, McCarthy Building, GNP Seguros deals
items.detail: Palantir (PLTR) traded between $107.88 and $114.08 on June 28, recovering from the 52-week low of $107.27 on June 25, though still roughly 35% below YTD high. AIPCon 10 showcased new enterprise AI platform deployments: a legal AI system with Kirkland & Ellis, an AI-powered operational system for McCarthy Building Companies, and a commercial insurance deployment with GNP Seguros in Mexico. Q1 2026 results posted $1.632B revenue (+84.7% YoY), the highest growth rate in company history.

items.headline: Treasury contract June 23 joins Army $10B and DHS $1B; Mythos restoration improves defense deployment outlook
items.detail: Palantir won a U.S. Treasury Department contract June 23 for a common API layer supporting developer platforms, workflow automation, and data analytics. 2026 federal contract wins: $10B 10-year Army enterprise AI services, $1B 5-year DHS AI platform, $300M USDA deal (April), and now Treasury. Mythos 5's partial restoration to critical infrastructure and government operators June 26 improves the outlook for the Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline strained since June 12. Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation.

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: iOS 27 with a fully rebuilt Siri AI powered by Google Gemini has been in developer beta since WWDC June 8; public beta expected mid-July with GA in September 2026. OpenAI retained outside counsel over Siri placement and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice. Apple is also evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option. The iOS 27 Extensions framework allowing user choice of ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini in Siri is disabled pending EU DMA clarity.

items.headline: iOS 27 with Gemini-powered Siri AI in developer beta since WWDC June 8; public beta mid-July; GA September 2026
items.detail: At WWDC June 8, Apple unveiled iOS 27 with a fully rebuilt Siri AI powered by Google's Gemini model, plus next-generation Apple Intelligence featuring personal context AI and Visual Intelligence. Siri AI features a dedicated standalone app, Dynamic Island integration, full on-screen awareness, and multi-step task execution, routed through a three-tier system: on-device, Private Cloud Compute, and Gemini for complex tasks. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri) is disabled pending EU DMA regulatory clarity. GA: September 2026.

items.headline: OpenAI retained outside counsel over Siri placement; Apple evaluating Mistral AI as additional model option
items.detail: OpenAI retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice against Apple over the perceived lack of prime ChatGPT placement in the new Siri AI, alleging Apple made 'no honest effort' to meet integration commitments. No lawsuit has been filed as of June 28. Sherwood News reports Apple is evaluating Mistral AI as an additional AI model option alongside Gemini, suggesting ongoing hedging across providers. Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions remain confirmed dead as of June 23.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: MI450 sampling is confirmed to lead customers OpenAI and Meta; Oracle announced a 50,000-unit MI450 deployment for the first publicly available AI supercluster in Q3 2026. MI450 specs: 432GB HBM4 memory and 20 TB/s bandwidth per GPU. AMD Advancing AI 2026 is July 22–23 at Moscone Center — expected to formally launch MI450, preview MI500, and reveal EPYC Venice Zen 6. Q2 2026 results report August 4.

items.headline: Oracle deploys 50,000 MI450 GPUs for public AI supercluster Q3 2026; MI450 samples shipping to OpenAI and Meta
items.detail: Oracle announced a 50,000-unit AMD Instinct MI450 GPU deployment for the first publicly available MI450-based AI supercluster, with the initial deployment in Q3 2026 and planned expansion in 2027. AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to lead customers OpenAI (6GW partnership, beginning with 1GW MI450 in H2 2026) and Meta ($60B/6GW). The MI450 features 432GB of HBM4 memory and 20 TB/s of bandwidth per GPU. Helios rack systems remain on H2 2026 schedule.

items.headline: AMD Advancing AI 2026 on July 22–23 at Moscone Center; MI500 engagement underway; Q2 results August 4
items.detail: AMD Advancing AI 2026 — July 22–23 at Moscone Center in San Francisco — is expected to formally launch MI450, preview EPYC Venice (Zen 6) CPUs, confirm Helios rack-scale systems, and offer a first look at the MI500 series targeting 2027. AMD CEO confirmed active customer engagement on MI500, noting that the largest AI deployments are increasingly for inference at scale. AMD recovered +2.60% on June 25 from the June 23 SK Hynix HBM4 selloff; analysts confirmed the H2 2026 MI450 production timeline is intact. Q2 2026 financial results: August 4.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: CoreWeave announced a multi-year partnership to power Perplexity's inference workloads on dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 clusters. CEO Aravind Srinivas maintains a 2028 IPO target. Comet AI browser is globally available on Mac and iOS, reaching #3 in the App Store within 48 hours of the iOS launch. Perplexity Computer runs as native side panels in Microsoft 365 apps. ARR at $500M (+335% YoY); 100M+ MAU; $20B valuation; $750M Azure deal.

items.headline: CoreWeave multi-year inference partnership powers Perplexity on dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 clusters
items.detail: CoreWeave announced a multi-year strategic partnership with Perplexity to support its inference workloads on dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72-powered clusters on CoreWeave Cloud. The agreement ensures Perplexity's infrastructure keeps pace with rapid growth and the sophisticated requirements of the Sonar and Search API ecosystem. CRWV shares jumped approximately 5.7% pre-market on the March 2026 announcement. CoreWeave will integrate NVIDIA Rubin-based systems beginning H2 2026, positioning the partnership for next-generation model serving.

items.headline: 2028 IPO target firm; Comet browser at #3 App Store; Perplexity Computer in Microsoft 365; $500M ARR
items.detail: CEO Aravind Srinivas reaffirmed a 2028 IPO target (June 9 CNBC), explicitly not accelerating despite Anthropic and OpenAI moving toward public markets. Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are confirmed dead as of June 23. The Comet AI browser reached #3 in the App Store within 48 hours of iOS launch and is globally available on Mac and iOS. Perplexity Computer runs as native side panels in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams. ARR: $500M (+335% YoY); 100M+ MAU; $20B valuation; $750M Azure deal.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: Russell 3000 inclusion became effective after market close June 26, triggering mandatory passive-fund buying; major institutions accelerated positions (Vanguard +275.6%). CRWV closed at $96.09 on June 28, with a $143.41 average analyst price target (37 analysts, 'Buy' consensus). Contracted backlog approaching $131 billion; 2026 capex floor $31 billion. The $3.1B HPC loan closed. Nine of the top ten AI model providers are CoreWeave customers.

items.headline: Russell 3000 effective June 26 — CRWV at $96.09 on June 28; Vanguard +275.6% stake; analyst avg target $143.41
items.detail: CoreWeave's Russell 3000 inclusion became effective after the June 26 market close, triggering mandatory passive-fund buying. Major institutions accelerated positions: Vanguard increased its stake by 275.6% to nearly 28 million shares (~$2 billion) and PNC Financial Services raised its stake by 248.9% in Q4. CRWV closed at $96.09 on June 28 (range $93.50–$98.09). Deutsche Bank maintained a Buy with a $135 price target; 37 analysts average 'Buy' with a $143.41 12-month price target.

items.headline: $3.1B HPC loan closed; backlog approaching $131B; 9 of top 10 AI labs on platform; Perplexity GB200 NVL72 partnership
items.detail: CoreWeave closed a $3.1 billion delayed draw term loan facility backed by its HPC infrastructure — described as the first public HPC infrastructure-backed financing structure for the AI cloud sector. Contracted backlog is approaching $131 billion, driven by Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout for Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI. Nine of the top ten AI model providers are CoreWeave customers. The Perplexity multi-year partnership adds dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 clusters for AI search inference. 2026 capex floor: $31 billion.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: Mistral AI's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris (44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems, operated by Eclairion) is in its final days of its opening window — the end-of-June target is 2 days away as of June 28. NVIDIA and Mistral launched Mistral Compute sovereign AI cloud at VivaTech (June 20). Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral for future Siri/iOS integration. CEO Mensch positions Mistral as the open-source sovereignty alternative amid the Fable ban. $830M debt facility secured.

items.headline: Paris data center end-of-June target 2 days away — 44MW, 13,800 GB300 GPUs, operated by Eclairion
items.detail: Mistral AI's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris — 44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems providing approximately 276 exaflops of FP4 compute, operated by Eclairion — is expected to open by end of June 2026, now 2 days away as of June 28. No official opening announcement has been made yet. A secondary Les Ulis (Essonne) 10 MW inference facility is scheduled for Q3 2026. The Bruyères-le-Châtel facility is the flagship of Mistral's planned 200 MW European compute roadmap.

items.headline: Mistral Compute launched at VivaTech with NVIDIA; Apple reportedly evaluating Mistral; CEO targets EUR 1B revenue
items.detail: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform built on 18,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell systems for European customers and governments — at VivaTech 2026 (June 20). Mistral is a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition for open frontier models. An $830M debt facility from Bpifrance, BNP Paribas, Credit Agricole CIB, HSBC, La Banque Postale, MUFG, and Natixis funds the buildout. Sherwood News reports Apple is evaluating Mistral for future Siri/iOS integration. CEO Mensch targets EUR 1B 2026 revenue with 1,000 employees.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: The Cybercab robotaxi fleet has approximately 59–100+ units operational across Austin metro, Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area, though the service faces criticism for long waits and stalled rides. Optimus Gen 3 is in mass production at Fremont; the Terafab initiative targets in-house AI5 chip production. Giga Texas North is targeting 10 million robots per year. Elon Musk told investors to be 'really patient' on both Optimus and Cybercab ramp timelines.

items.headline: Cybercab fleet ~59–100+ units across four markets; long waits and stalled rides persist; Musk urges patience
items.detail: Tesla's Cybercab fleet has approximately 59 to 100+ units operating across the full Austin metro area (expanded June 8), Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area. The service faces criticism for long passenger wait times and stalled rides. EPA filing (June 19) confirmed production Cybercab specs: 47.6 kWh battery, 163 kW motor, 418-mile unadjusted range (293-mile adjusted), 3,113 lb curb weight. Elon Musk told investors June 20 to be 'really patient' with the ramp. Infrastructure expansion is underway including robocar car washes and charging hubs in Texas.

items.headline: Optimus Gen 3 in mass production at Fremont; Terafab AI5 chip initiative; 10M robot/year Giga Texas North target
items.detail: Tesla's Optimus Gen 3 humanoid robot is in mass production at Fremont, with V3 full-body production targeting summer 2026. Tesla announced Terafab — an in-house semiconductor fabrication initiative for the AI5 chip powering Optimus Gen 3 — on March 21. The Gigafactory Texas North Campus expansion (5.2 million square feet) targets 10 million robots per year. Approximately 300–500 total Optimus units currently exist in learning and data-collection phase. FSD v14.3 is deployed with 20% reduced inference latency.

ipoTracker.status: Mythos 5 partially restored June 26 to ~100 companies and agencies; Fable 5 still suspended Day 16; Axios reports Fable access 'as soon as this coming week'; AI for Science event June 30 (2 days); $30B+ Series H closing at >$900B valuation; October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target
ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confirmed its confidential draft Form S-1 submission to the SEC on June 1, 2026, targeting an October 2026 Nasdaq IPO at a projected ~$1.1 trillion first-day market cap. The company is closing a Series H round exceeding $30 billion at a valuation above $900 billion. As of June 28 (Day 16), Fable 5 remains suspended for general use. Mythos 5 was partially restored June 26 to approximately 100 trusted companies and federal agencies. Axios reported June 27 that the Trump administration is close to allowing Fable 5 access 'as soon as this coming week,' pending Pentagon and NSA sign-off. John Jumper (Nobel laureate) joined from Google DeepMind; AI for Science event is June 30. Anthropic's revenue run rate exceeds $30 billion.
ipoTracker.note: June 30 AI for Science event and the imminent Fable 5 restoration are the next public milestones. July 8 privacy policy update tied to biometric ID restoration pathway for broader US access.

latestArticle.title: Trump admin allows Anthropic to release Mythos AI model to some companies, government agencies

ipoTracker.status: GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna launched June 26 under government restrictions; IPO leaning 2027; 42-state AG probe ongoing; ChatGPT market share below 50%
ipoTracker.process: OpenAI filed a confidential S-1 with the SEC on June 8, 2026, with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan leading. The company is reportedly leaning toward a 2027 listing — CFO Sarah Friar cited operational readiness concerns, Stargate cash burn, and SpaceX IPO volatility dynamics. GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna launched June 26 in limited preview under U.S. government-imposed access restrictions. GPT-4.5 was retired June 26. A coalition of 42 state AGs opened a formal probe June 12. ChatGPT's global market share fell to 46.4% — first time below 50%.
ipoTracker.note: GPT-5.6 government restrictions are the key product development to watch. The 2027 IPO lean and 42-state AG probe are the dominant pre-IPO risk factors. EU AI Act chatbot enforcement begins August 2, 2026.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI releases powerful new GPT-5.6 model under restrictions

ipoTracker.status: SPCX ~$153 (June 28, -32% from ATH of $225.64 on June 16); Grok 4.5 private beta at Tesla and SpaceX; Reflection AI $150M/month starts July 1 (3 days); $60B Cursor acquisition proceeding Q3 close; CSAM concerns on Grok adult content
ipoTracker.process: SpaceX priced at $135/share on June 11, raising $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation in the largest IPO ever recorded. Shares peaked at $225.64 on June 16 following the Cursor announcement, then fell to approximately $153 by June 28 (-32% from ATH). Grok 4.5 entered private beta at Tesla and SpaceX this week (1.5 trillion parameters, V9 model, Cursor training data). Both Colossus 1 (Anthropic ~$1.25B/month) and Colossus 2 (Reflection AI, starting July 1) are committed to third parties. The $60B Cursor acquisition is on track for Q3 2026 close. xAI engineers acknowledged CSAM is an unsolved technical problem in Grok's image generation.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 12, 2026
ipoTracker.note: Reflection AI $150M/month starts July 1 — 3 days away. Grok CSAM disclosure is a new regulatory and reputational risk. Cursor Q3 close is the next corporate milestone.

latestArticle.title: Grok 4.5 enters private beta at SpaceX and Tesla with 1.5 trillion parameters

ipoTracker.status: CBRS recovering after post-earnings selloff; Q1 2026 revenue $193.4M (3x estimates); $20B+ OpenAI compute deal; AWS inference disaggregation partnership active
ipoTracker.process: Cerebras completed its Nasdaq IPO in May 2026 under ticker CBRS. Q1 2026 results released June 23 massively beat estimates: $193.4M revenue vs. $56.65M expected (+92–94% YoY). Two major deals announced alongside earnings: multi-year OpenAI compute deal (750MW, $20B+ valued, option to $30B) and a multi-year AWS partnership pairing Cerebras CS-3 (decode) with AWS Trainium 3 (prefill). CBRS closed at $168.52 on June 25 (-7.61%) on forward gross margin compression guidance.
ipoTracker.note: CBRS post-earnings margin guidance selloff is the near-term risk. The $20B+ OpenAI deal establishes Cerebras as a Tier 1 AI compute provider alongside CoreWeave.

latestArticle.title: Cerebras Systems Announces Strong First Quarter 2026 Results

ipoTracker.status: CRWV ~$96.09 on June 28; Russell 3000 effective June 26; institutional buying surge (Vanguard +275%); analyst avg target $143.41; backlog ~$131B; $3.1B HPC loan closed
ipoTracker.process: CoreWeave completed its Nasdaq IPO in March 2025 (priced at $40/share) and trades as CRWV. Russell 3000 inclusion became effective after June 26 close, triggering mandatory passive-fund buying. CRWV closed at $96.09 on June 28 (range $93.50–$98.09). Q1 2026 revenue was $2.1 billion (+112% YoY). Contracted backlog approaching $131 billion. The $3.1B HPC loan recently closed. Nine of the top ten AI model providers are customers. 2026 capex floor raised to $31 billion.
ipoTracker.note: Russell 3000 now effective. CRWV at $96 vs $143 analyst average target. Vanguard and other institutional investors significantly increased positions.

The company has a $5.4 billion ARR growing 65% YoY with positive free cash flow. SpaceXAI announced native Grok availability on Databricks Agent Bricks at the 2026 Data + AI Summit (June 18).

ipoTracker.note: CEO's tone has shifted toward IPO-bound. Strong fundamentals support a $165–175B valuation. Watch for S-1 filing announcement.

The IPO was widely seen as an open-source AI watershed — the first major public listing of a company whose primary assets are open-source model repositories, training infrastructure, and developer community.

partnershipTracker.type: Custom AI silicon partnership — Jalapeño LLM inference accelerator
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed — Jalapeño unveiled June 24; LLM inference ASIC built in 9 months on TSMC advanced process; H2 2026 Stargate deployment target
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI and Broadcom unveiled Jalapeño on June 24 — OpenAI's first custom AI accelerator, a reticle-sized ASIC designed from the ground up for LLM inference. Co-developed from initial design to manufacturing tape-out in nine months — the companies describe this as the fastest ASIC development cycle ever achieved in high-performance advanced semiconductors. Broadcom manufactures Jalapeño on TSMC's advanced process. Early testing shows substantially better performance per watt than current state-of-the-art. Initial deployment targets H2 2026 in Stargate data centers.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies
partnershipTracker.note: Jalapeño complements NVIDIA GPUs in Stargate for specific large-scale LLM inference workloads. H2 2026 Stargate deployment timeline is the key milestone.

partnershipTracker.type: AI compute contract — Colossus 2 data center access
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed $6.3B deal — $150M/month starts July 1 (3 days away); Colossus 2 dedicated to Reflection AI
partnershipTracker.terms: SpaceX signed a $6.3 billion compute agreement with Reflection AI (valued at $25 billion, working with DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI), providing access to NVIDIA GB300 GPUs via the Colossus 2 data center in Memphis. Reflection pays $150 million per month starting July 1 (3 days away) through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after the first 3 months. Combined with Colossus 1 rented to Anthropic, this confirms SpaceX operates Colossus as a commercial multi-tenant AI data center platform.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by CNBC reporting
partnershipTracker.note: July 1 start date is 3 days away. Grok 4.5 entering private beta at SpaceX alongside Reflection AI's Colossus 2 commitment raises ongoing questions about Grok AI's own dedicated compute access.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX AI Colossus data center signs deal with Reflection AI

partnershipTracker.type: Structured finance platform for Broadcom AI XPU deployment
partnershipTracker.status: Operational — $35B capital solution; enables 20GW+ Broadcom XPU compute; Jalapeño chip reinforces Broadcom's position as ASIC partner for frontier AI labs; dividend payable June 30
partnershipTracker.terms: Apollo and Blackstone are leading a $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform, enabling more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute capacity using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. The platform is operational since June 9. The OpenAI-Broadcom Jalapeño chip announcement (June 24) confirms OpenAI as a Broadcom custom-silicon customer, reinforcing Broadcom's position across multiple frontier AI labs. Broadcom's $0.65 quarterly dividend is payable June 30 — 2 days away.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by Apollo press release and Broadcom announcement
partnershipTracker.note: Jalapeño chip confirms OpenAI is Broadcom's sixth custom-silicon customer. Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance ~$16B.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and Broadcom reveal Jalapeno, first AI chip in partnership
